projectName: PARQ Integration to One Bangkok Application
summary: Stakeholder portal generated from repository source artifacts. Simon readiness and UX-impact assessments are now reflected; no new requirements are created here.

areas.status: Current Working Baseline with Bas confirmations recorded
areas.notes: Core user-flow, proposal, clarification, BMS, domain, and system reference artifacts are filed. Platform/service ownership is confirmed for key systems; named human contacts remain needed for SIT/UAT escalation.
Architecture review remains reference; Molly UX stakeholder flow pack is the current draft Discovery input with open parking, tower, notification, lifecycle, and traceability questions.

areas.status: Current Working Baseline / Review with Blockers
areas.notes: Architecture, dependency, visual flow, BMS impact, readiness reassessment, and Molly UX impact assessment artifacts are available. Developer technical review can start with blockers; targeted updates remain for UF-010, UF-012, UF-006, UF-007, UF-014, and UF-004.

areas.status: No standalone delivery artifact currently identified
areas.notes: Delivery folder exists for accepted delivery references.

areas.status: Ready for QA readiness planning with blockers
areas.notes: Quinn can start environment dependency, contact-request, test-data request, and system readiness registers. Detailed SIT/UAT/negative/regression matrix still waits for contracts, environments, data, named contacts, fallback rules, audit, and reconciliation details.

highlights: BMS Option B non-blocking login refresh decision is recorded.
SIT/UAT/PVT readiness depends on vendor endpoints, data, and hardware paths.
Proposal PDF source is now available and registered.
Platform/service ownership is confirmed for IAM, BMS, FS/Iviva, BZB, Argento, CMS, Kafka/Event Bus, Notification, Elevator, and Turnstile.
Named human contact persons are still required for SIT/UAT escalation.
Simon readiness reassessment records 70% readiness for QA readiness planning and 45% readiness for final SIT/UAT execution planning.
Developer technical review can start with blockers based on the Molly UX impact assessment.
Quinn is the recommended next owner for QA readiness/environment dependency registers, while Simon owns targeted architecture update follow-up.
